description:
Goal
Make the activation-partition model documented, observable, and mechanically protected before ReturnEvidence lands on top of it. After this ticket, `ActivationKey` means identity partition only, and `ActivationInputs` means joined body evidence; no doc, test, or helper may blur that boundary.

Signal
- `.agent/docs/fact-engine.md`, `.agent/docs/semantic-fixpoint.md`, `.agent/docs/type-specialization.md`, `.agent/docs/type-world.md`, telemetry docs, and any guide/northstar-facing text describe the code's actual current model: current vs settled fact uses, activation identity partition, joined input evidence, recursive return ascent, and meaningful-event budgets.
- Grep-level checks prove stale concepts are gone or explicitly ticketed: `convergence_class`, recursive-only key widening, `ActivationKey` as body input, raw closure-capture key defaults, missing return as `none`, unresolved return as `any`, `ReturnType` as readiness, stale `FactValue::Inputs` docs, debug telemetry payload budgets, and unbounded `fz2 --log-telemetry` workflows.
- Quicksort/Enum convergence telemetry budgets are documented with measured post-fix numbers and hard-timeout commands.
- No temporary construction fixtures, ignored tests, or local affordances from `fz-rh2.18.9.5` remain without a blocking ticket.

Strategy
- Update docs only after the `fz-rh2.18.9.5` code and tests are verified; docs must cite the implemented model, not the intended future.
- Replace stale prose instead of layering caveats. If the code has changed, delete the old model wording.
- Add or run no-go searches as part of the closeout; every survivor is either deleted here or becomes a new blocker before `fz-rh2.18.6` starts.
- Reconcile any target/plan docs that still describe bridge/shim/scaffold work as acceptable; if a future pull-product epic is needed, ticket it explicitly instead of hiding it in activation-partition docs.

Outro
No stale activation-key/body-input docs, no renamed `convergence_class`, no compatibility shim preserving old key semantics, no missing/unresolved evidence encoded as type top/bottom, no leftover ignored convergence tests, no temporary telemetry budget that ignores event explosions, and no bridge/scaffold language presented as the accepted path for this arc.
title: Compiler2 activation partitioning closeout: docs and deletion contract
